person.userCertifiedLifeSummary:
Louis de Scarpone or Mousson, born about 1015, died between 1071 and 1076, was Count of Montbéliard, Altkirch and Ferrette (which means that some authors call him count of Sundgau) and by his marriage Count of Bar and Lord of Monsoon.

«b»Biography«/b»
Sophie and her sister Beatrice, daughters of Frederick II., Duke of Upper Lotharingia, had been collected in 1033 by their maternal aunt, Gisela of Swabia , wife of the Germanic emperor Conrad II Salic. Lorraine (or Upper Lorraine ) was entrusted to Count Verdun Gothelon I st, but the emperor wanting strengthen its influence in Verdun front of the house that was soon show signs of agitation, married his niece Sophie Louis in 1038. This marriage brought him the county of Barand the lordship of Mousson.

A little later, in 1042, the Emperor gave him the counties of Montbéliard, Altkirch and Ferrette. In 1044, he successfully fought Renaud I st, Count of Burgundy who had revolted against the emperor, with Count Gerold Geneva.

In 1047, the Duke of Lorraine Godefroy II had revolted and had been defeated. The Lorraine, confiscated, had been given to Adalbert of Alsace, then to Gerard of Alsace, who died in 1070. Because his wife was the daughter of the first dukes of Lorraine, Louis claimed the duchy, but the Emperor Henry IV decided in favor of Thierry II, Gerard's son. This dispute was the origin of the rivalry between the Counts of Bar and the dukes of Lorraine, rivalry will disappear in 1420, with René of Anjou, Isabella  I re Lorraine and the union of the two houses.

Louis died shortly afterwards.

«b»Family«/b» 
«b»Ancestry «/b»
He was the son of Richwin, Count of Scarpone and Mathilde of Eguisheim, the sister of Pope Leo IX .

«b»Marriage and Descent «/b»
From his wife Sophie de Bar , he had:

1.) Bruno, Young Death

2.) Thierry (ca.1045-1103), Count of Montbéliard, Bar and Mousson

3.) Louis, cited in 1080

4.) Frédéric (1092), Marquis of Suze

5.) Mathilde (1091/1105), married to Hugh of Dagsburg († 1089)

6.) Sophie, married to Folmar, Earl of Froburg

7.) Beatrice (1092), married to Berthold I st of Zähringen (1078), Duke of Carinthia

zh.lifeSummary: 當Louis de Montbéliard-Mousson於從1015年到1019年出生在法國阿爾薩斯豪吉爾豪森時，他的父親Richwin de Charpeigne-Mousson24歲，母親Hildegard von Egisheim21歲。他和Sophie de Haute Lorraine de Lotharingia至少有4個兒子和3個女兒。他去世於從1071年8月29日到1076年8月29日在法國福朗-可特艾度布斯蒙特貝利亞德，埋葬在法國豪-阿爾培斯諾特-達。

zh-hans.lifeSummary: 当Louis de Montbéliard-Mousson于从1015年到1019年出生在法国阿爾薩斯豪吉爾豪森时，他的父亲Richwin de Charpeigne-Mousson24岁，母亲Hildegard von Egisheim21岁。他和Sophie de Haute Lorraine de Lotharingia至少有4个儿子和3个女儿。他去世于从1071年8月29日到1076年8月29日在法国福朗-可特艾度布斯蒙特貝利亞德，埋葬在法国豪-阿爾培斯諾特-達。

nameMeanings.name: de
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Indian Dilip, Anirban, Gopal, Alok, Anindya, Arunava, Asit, Asok, Dibyendu, Goutam, Indra, Indranil.

variants.name: Dey
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Indian Ashit, Indu, Naina, Partha, Soumya, Ajit, Anjan, Anjana, Anup, Arjun, Arun, Ashim.
English, Scottish, and Welsh: variant of   Day  .
German: topographic name from a field name in North Rhine-Westphalia, denoting a sizeable piece of land, from Middle Low German dē ‘thigh’ (referring to the shape of the property).

nameMeanings.name: Louis
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of First Names © Patrick Hanks and Flavia Hodges 1990, 2003, 2006.
meanings: name, of Germanic (Frankish) origin, from hlōd ‘fame’ + wīg ‘war’. It was very common in French royal and noble families. Louis I ( 778–840 ) was the son of Charlemagne, who ruled as both King of France and Holy Roman Emperor. Altogether, the name was borne by sixteen kings of France up to the French Revolution, in which Louis XVI perished. Louis XIV, ‘the Sun King’ ( 1638–1715 ), reigned for seventy-two years ( 1643–1715 ), presiding in the middle part of his reign over a period of unparalleled French power and prosperity. In modern times Louis is also found in the English-speaking world (usually pronounced ‘loo-ee’). In Britain the Anglicized form Lewis is rather more common, whereas in America the reverse is true.

variants.name: Lewie
meanings: Anglicized spelling of   Louis  , or a pet form of   Lewis .

variants.name: Louie
meanings: Variant spelling of   Lewie   or   Louis  , currently in fashion.

variants.name: Lewi
meanings: form, since the Middle Ages, of the French name Louis . In modern use it is also in part a transferred use of the surname derived from this given name, or from Anglicized forms of Gaelic Mac Lughaidh ‘son of Lughaidh ’ or Welsh Llewelyn .

en.lifeSummary: When Louis de Montbéliard-Mousson was born from 1015 to 1019, in Houssen, Haut-Rhin, Alsace, France, his father, Richwin de Charpeigne-Mousson, was 24 and his mother, Hildegard von Egisheim, was 21. He had at least 4 sons and 3 daughters with Sophie de Haute Lorraine de Lotharingia. He died from 29 August 1071 to 29 August 1076, in Montbéliard, Doubs, Franche-Comté, France, and was buried in Notre-Dame, Hautes-Alpes, France.

fr.lifeSummary: Lorsque Louis de Montbéliard-Mousson est né du 1015 au 1019, à Houssen, Haut-Rhin, Alsace, France, son père, Richwin de Charpeigne-Mousson, avait 24 ans et sa mère, Hildegard von Egisheim, avait 21 ans. Il a eu au moins 4 fils et 3 filles avec Sophie de Haute Lorraine de Lotharingia. Il est décédé du 29 août 1071 au 29 août 1076, à Montbéliard, Doubs, Franche-Comté, France, et a été enterré à Notre-Dame, Hautes-Alpes, France.

de.lifeSummary: Als Louis de Montbéliard-Mousson von 1015 bis 1019 in Houssen, Haut-Rhin, Elsaß, Frankreich geboren wurde, war sein Vater Richwin de Charpeigne-Mousson 24 und seine Mutter Hildegard von Egisheim 21. Er hatte mit Sophie de Haute Lorraine de Lotharingia mindestens 4 Söhne und 3 Töchter. Er starb von 29. August 1071 bis 29. August 1076 in Montbéliard, Doubs, Franche-Comté, Frankreich und wurde in Notre-Dame, Hautes-Alpes, Frankreich begraben.

it.lifeSummary: Quando Louis de Montbéliard-Mousson è nato il da 1015 a 1019 nel Houssen, Haut-Rhin, Alsace, Francia, il suo padre, Richwin de Charpeigne-Mousson, aveva 24 anni e il suo madre, Hildegard von Egisheim, aveva 21 anni. Lui avuto almeno 4 figli e 3 figlie con Sophie de Haute Lorraine de Lotharingia. Morì da 29 agosto 1071 a 29 agosto 1076, nel Montbéliard, Doubs, Franche-Comté, Francia, e fu sepolto a Notre-Dame, Hautes-Alpes, Francia.

ja.lifeSummary: Louis de Montbéliard-Moussonがから1015年まで1019年にフランスアルサスハウト-ルヒンホーウセンで生まれた時，父Richwin de Charpeigne-Moussonは24歳，母Hildegard von Egisheimは21歳であった。彼はSophie de Haute Lorraine de Lotharingiaとの間に少なくとも4人の息子と3人の娘をもうけた。彼はから1071年8月29日まで1076年8月29日にフランスフランチェ-コムトeドウブズモントブeリアルドで死去し，フランスハウテス-アルペスノトレ-ダムにて埋葬された。

ko.lifeSummary: Louis de Montbéliard-Mousson(이)가 1015년 부터 1019년 까지 프랑스 알사스 하트-히 허센에서 태어났을 때, 그의 아버지 Richwin de Charpeigne-Mousson(은)는 24세였고, 그의 어머니 Hildegard von Egisheim(은)는 21세였다. 그는 Sophie de Haute Lorraine de Lotharingia(와)과 적어도 네 명의 아들과 세 명의 딸을 두었다. 그는 1071년 8월 29일부터 1076년 8월 29일까지 프랑스 프랭체-코트e 다브스 몽브e리아르드에서 사망했으며 프랑스 하츠-알프스 노트레-다에 묻혔다.

pt.lifeSummary: Quando Louis de Montbéliard-Mousson nasceu de 1015 a 1019, em Houssen, Haut-Rhin, Alsace, França, seu pai, Richwin de Charpeigne-Mousson, tinha 24 anos e sua mãe, Hildegard von Egisheim, tinha 21 anos. Ele teve pelo menos 4 filhos e 3 filhas com Sophie de Haute Lorraine de Lotharingia. Ele faleceu de 29 de agosto de 1071 a 29 de agosto de 1076, em Montbéliard, Doubs, Franche-Comté, França, e foi sepultado em Notre-Dame, Hautes-Alpes, França.

ru.lifeSummary: Когда Louis de Montbéliard-Mousson родился с 1015 по 1019 в местности хоуссен, хаут-рин, алсеис, Франция, его отцу по имени Richwin de Charpeigne-Mousson было 24 года, а матери, которую звали Hildegard von Egisheim, 21. У него было по крайней мере 4 сына и 3 дочери от Sophie de Haute Lorraine de Lotharingia. Он умер с 29 августа 1071 по 29 августа 1076 в местности монтбeлярд, доубс, фрэнче-комтe, Франция и был похоронен в местности нотре-дэм, хаутес-алпесь, Франция.
